rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical cyclopropanation of alkenes with diethyl malonate using a ferrocene mediator to form aryl-substituted cyclopropane-1,1-dicarboxylates
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation mediated by ferrocene (Fc/Fc+) generates a malonyl radical from diethyl malonate; the radical adds to the tethered alkene and after further oxidation the resulting carbanion (Int3) undergoes intramolecular SN2-type ring closure displacing iodide

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical adduct after addition to alkene
TEMPO adduct (compound 91) of the C-radical intermediate
iodinated malonate intermediate Int3 (anion after deprotonation)

electron_flow: Ferrocene is oxidized at the anode to Fc+, which oxidizes diethyl malonate to a malonyl radical; this radical adds across the tethered alkene to give a C-radical that is further oxidized/iodinated and deprotonated to Int3, which closes via SN2-type displacement of iodide to form the cyclopropane.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO adduct 91 (C16H29NO5, HRMS m/z 316.2124) was detected, and TEMPO-malonate adduct 92 was also observed; desired cyclopropane product was not detected, supporting a radical intermediate

notes: DFT (SMD(DMA/H2O)/ωB97M-V/def2-QZVPP//IEFPCM/M06-2X-D3/ma-SVP) supports an SN2-type ring closure from Int3 (ΔG = -29.7 kcal/mol, ΔG‡ = 7.3 kcal/mol) as thermodynamically and kinetically facile. Theoretical oxidation potentials were calculated to justify the Fc mediator (vs. SHE, with E_SHE^abs = 4.280 V). Alternative non-mediated direct anodic oxidation pathway was considered but mediator pathway is favored.
